reviews.review_description: Title aside, this is a great app.  However, the previous reviewers really weren’t kidding about the app not supporting anything other than OEM flows.  Like theres no free play mode so if it’s not an OEM flow the button won’t move.  If your airline uses OEM flows this is gonna be a 12 star app.  If not unfortunately it’s just an expensive wall poster.  Still useful, just maybe not worth the money

As I really do love this app as personally I actually love aviation at this game is used by some big airlines to train pilots and they offer a airbus a320 one a 737 ng one and a 737-8 one to and some more but I think if you like plans and wonder how real world pilots train ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Very useful! a little bit better than a paper tiger. If you could somehow program it to be able to move switches in the “explore mode” that would be awesome. That way I can study for my specific airline flows. Something like just having the switches manipulate the system with ground power or IDG power would make this a 1000 better. having a “simulator mode” so we can manipulate the flight deck as necessary is pretty much what I’m getting at.

Very well designed and helpful for initial type class, i very enjoyed the app
Wish had more procedures but still good to have something to practice

reviews.review_title: Great use of VR for airline training!
reviews.review_description: Having previously used their A320 sim extensively, this B737 sim is just as good as their prior offering. All normal procedures and five emergency procedures are represented here. Buttons can be toggled, majority of the displays are simulated, and step-by-step instructions are available for the whole process. In addition, the sim offers an Explore Mode where you can read more info about specific buttons and switches. The developer has stated that the sim will continue to receive updates so this will be a good training investment. If you wanna try it out before purchasing, check out the demos for this and the A320 sim also listed on App Lab. This sim isn't a replacement for a full featured sim like MSFS2020 or X-Plane, but it's a nice companion that offers portability of not needing a full gaming PC and the ability to train anywhere, even while on travel.
